mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly supporting higher equity valuations and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% over consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below the 8.0 percent threshold signals labor market tightening that boosts consumer confidence and corporate profit margins, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain flat or negative in 70% of months where UNRATE crosses below 8.0% from above.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer spending strength, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between the joint RSAFS-HOUST momentum signal and 60-day forward returns falls below 0.05 over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery regime reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, generating short-term positive market momentum.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power (IC < 0.02) when applied outside the post-shock recovery regime or during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the consumer price index above 2.5 percent triggers expectations of central bank tightening, which increases discount rates and compresses equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in 65% of instances when CPIAUCSL exceeds the 235.0 threshold, contradicting the tightening narrative.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and rising wage bills, which supports corporate earnings growth and yields positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The payroll growth signal produces negative or zero alpha over 60-day horizons in 80% of observation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales reveals underlying demand fragility, where slowing consumer spending outpaces macro output, forecasting negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3.0% annually, showing regime dependency not captured in the model.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production coinciding with rising inflation signals supply-side bottlenecks that erode profit margins and trigger short-term risk-off positioning in equity markets.
failure_condition: The joint supply-inflation signal generates positive 5-day returns in 70% of triggered cases, invalidating the margin-erosion mechanism.
